Model identifier 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' is an Anthropic model; no OpenAI SDK or 'gpt-4o-mini' references remain.

length_sanity.note: Estimated word count (~3250 prose words, ~3500 including code blocks) is within the standard profile range (3000–4400). PASS.

topic_scope.boundary: The 5-role context framework — Authority, Exemplar, Constraint, Rubric, Metadata
topic_scope.note: Corpus stays on the 5-role framework, its sub-concepts, and its Python implementation throughout. No teaching of RAG, agents, multi-agent systems, or other advanced adjacent topics. Resource [3] ('context-engineered AI prompt framework for AI agents') is used only as an applied example citation — the corpus does not teach agent concepts. Section 9 correctly maps roles back to prior topics (13.1–13.6). PASS.

undefined_terms.note: Python f-string syntax used without a definition. However, given that Week 12 topics established Anthropic SDK usage (implying Python fluency), and f-strings are a Python fundamental introduced earlier in M5, this is acceptable in context. One soft finding does not reach the ≥2-cold-terms threshold for a soft-fail.

beginner_readability.note: LLM spelled out on first use ('large language model (LLM)', line 47). All five roles defined on first use with everyday analogy before formal definition. Sentences are short and active-voice throughout. No hedging phrases. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. 1 soft finding (f-string) — well below the ≥3 escalation threshold. PASS.

attempt_1_failures_resolved.original_finding: Section 5 role definitions (Roles 1–5), priority table, and missing-role consequence list carried no citation markers; Sections 6 and 7 also below threshold; overall density ~25%.
attempt_1_failures_resolved.resolution: Each role block now has [1] at its priority-position statement; priority table covered by [1] at line 128; Section 7 Mistakes 1, 3, 4, 5 now carry [1] or [2]; Section 6 and 8 carry [3]. Block-level density in Section 5 is now 80%.

attempt_1_failures_resolved.original_finding: Section 8 step 8 used `import openai`, `openai.chat.completions.create()`, and model `gpt-4o-mini` — OpenAI SDK never introduced in curriculum.
attempt_1_failures_resolved.resolution: Section 8 step 8 now uses `import anthropic`, `anthropic.Anthropic()`, `client.messages.create()`, and model `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` — consistent with taught curriculum interface.

verdict: PASSED — all six checks pass on second attempt. Both first-attempt hard failures have been resolved: (1) citation density in Sections 5, 7, and 8 now meets the 60% floor (block-level density in Section 5 is 80%; Section 7 is 80%; Section 8 procedural steps are appropriately cited); (2) forward reference to the OpenAI SDK has been replaced with the Anthropic SDK pattern used throughout the curriculum. Required sections present and non-trivial. Length within standard range (~3250 words). No scope creep. Beginner readability passes with one negligible soft finding (f-string undefined — acceptable given established Python context from Week 12). Corpus is ready to ground downstream artifacts.
